page: Amazon Web Services x2gd.large is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 model running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between memory capacity and raw computational throughput. While stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls within the average range at 1519.29, multi-core CPU performance is weak at 3013.58. Memory bandwidth tests show 117.29 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 36.51 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Geekbench results reflect lower-tier processing capabilities, with a single-core score of 946.0 and a multi-core score of 1725.0. Database operations under Passmark score at 1296.65, and Redis SET operations achieve 2.32 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x2gd.large offers a high density of memory and local NVMe storage relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU performance. The presence of local NVMe SSD storage supports fast read and write access for temporary data. This server type is suitable for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, lightweight database operations, and basic web serving workloads that benefit from a large memory footprint on ARM-based architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.large is an arm64 memory-optimized instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores. It features 32.0 GB of memory, providing a high ratio of 16.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals a tradeoff, with average single-core CPU performance but lower multi-core and memory bandwidth scores, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 1725.0 and Redis SET throughput of 2.32 million operations per second. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and lightweight database operations that require a substantial memory footprint and local SSD storage without demanding high-compute performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.large is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 2 Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive workloads and caching.

tagline: An ARM-based memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high memory density, and local NVMe SSD storage.

Equipped with 32.0 GB of memory providing 16.0 GB per core
